messages.author: swarm
messages.text: This ticket needs your approval before it can proceed. Reply here with "approve" to approve it.

messages.author: swarm
messages.text:
What it is: Cap the vitest worker pool and per-worker heap so a test run cannot OOM-spiral the swarm
What it solves: WHAT / WHY. A plain `vitest run` (and the acceptance runs that shell it) sizes
its worker pool to the host CPU count and sets no per-worker heap limit
(extension/vitest.config.mjs today caps NOTHING — no pool, no poolOptions, no
--max-old-space-size). One run ballooned to ~13GB and started an OOM-s…
How it works: The vitest configuration exposes an explicit finite maximum worker count rather than the CPU-count default

messages.author: swarm
messages.text: BL-422 - Cap the vitest worker pool and per-worker heap so a test run cannot OOM-spiral the swarm is complete.
